eras.label: Cenozoic
eras.desc: Rise of mammals and flowering plants.

periods.name: Quaternary
periods.desc: Repeated ice ages occur. Humans evolve; modern ecosystems and climate develop.

epochs.name: Holocene
epochs.desc: Age of humans — civilisation, agriculture and rapid climate change.
epochs.imgDesc: Çatalhöyük — Neolithic farming settlement

epochs.name: Pleistocene
epochs.desc: Ice ages; megafauna extinction and early humans.
epochs.imgDesc: Woolly mammoths, rhinos, horses, and cave lions roamed.

periods.name: Neogene
periods.desc: Mammals diversify on land and sea.

epochs.name: Pliocene
epochs.desc: Early human ancestors evolved; bipedalism and stone tools emerged.
epochs.imgDesc: Australopithecus anamensis — earliest known hominin

epochs.name: Miocene
epochs.desc: Apes evolved and diversified; human–chimp lineage split in the late epoch.
epochs.imgDesc: Apes evolved and diversified, with the human–chimp lineage splitting late.

periods.name: Paleogene
periods.desc: Primitive mammals and birds flourish.

epochs.name: Oligocene
epochs.desc: Earth cools sharply; grasslands spread as modern mammals establish.
epochs.imgDesc: Dogs, nimravids, bears, weasels, and raccoons replaced the creodonts.

epochs.name: Eocene
epochs.desc: Warm greenhouse Earth; modern mammals rapidly diversify.
epochs.imgDesc: Hoofed animals diversified in a major radiation between Europe and North America.

epochs.name: Paleocene
epochs.desc: Mammals quickly diversified, filling roles left by extinct dinosaurs.
epochs.imgDesc: Purgatorius — earliest known primate

eras.label: Mesozoic
eras.desc: Age of dinosaurs. First birds appear.

periods.name: Cretaceous
periods.desc: First flowering plants; dinosaurs go extinct.

epochs.name: Late Cretaceous
epochs.desc: Dinosaurs reach their peak; K–Pg extinction event.
epochs.imgDesc: K–Pg extinction wiped out ~75–80% of species, including all non-avian dinosaurs.

epochs.name: Early Cretaceous
epochs.desc: First flowering plants; ceratopsians, spinosaurids and coelurosaurs emerge.
epochs.imgDesc: Spinosaurids (shown here), Ceratopsians, Carcharodontosaurids and Coelurosaurs emerged.

periods.name: Jurassic
periods.desc: Dinosaurs dominant; first bird fossils.

epochs.name: Late Jurassic
epochs.desc: Famous dinosaur groups thrived; the first birds appeared.
epochs.imgDesc: Theropods (shown here), Sauropods and Thyreophorans emerged.

epochs.name: Middle Jurassic
epochs.desc: Pangaea begins to split; mammals rapidly diversify.
epochs.imgDesc: Brachiosaurs (shown here), Cetiosaurs, Megalosaurs and primitive Ornithopods emerged.

epochs.name: Early Jurassic
epochs.desc: Dinosaurs diversify; marine reptiles and early mammals flourish.
epochs.imgDesc: Ammonites explosively diversified after near-extinction.

periods.name: Triassic
periods.desc: First mammals and dinosaurs emerge.

epochs.name: Late Triassic
epochs.desc: First dinosaurs emerge; Triassic–Jurassic extinction event.
epochs.imgDesc: Herrerasaurus (shown here), Plateosaurus, Coelophysis, and Eoraptor emerged.

epochs.name: Middle Triassic
epochs.desc: Marine reptiles diversify; Pangaea begins drifting.
epochs.imgDesc: Mixosaurus panxianensis — early ichthyosaur.

epochs.name: Early Triassic
epochs.desc: Extinction recovery; first archosaurs, crocodile and dinosaur ancestors emerge.
epochs.imgDesc: Erythrosuchus — one of the first archosauriforms.

eras.label: Paleozoic
eras.desc: Explosion of complex life. Sea to land.

periods.name: Permian
periods.desc: Reptiles thrive; greatest mass extinction.

epochs.name: Lopingian
epochs.desc: End-Permian extinction; ~96% of species lost.
epochs.imgDesc: Gorgonops — top predator before extinction

epochs.name: Guadalupian
epochs.desc: Therapsids dominate; two extinction events devastate life.
epochs.imgDesc: Titanophoneus — top predator of the Guadalupian.

epochs.name: Cisuralian
epochs.desc: Synapsids rule the land; beetles and flies appear.
epochs.imgDesc: Dimetrodon — a pelycosaur, early reptile-like synapsid.

periods.name: Carboniferous
periods.desc: Coal swamp forests; giant insects.
periods.imgCredit: Mary Evans Picture Library — Public domain

epochs.name: Pennsylvanian
epochs.desc: Amniote egg evolves — tetrapods reproduce on land for the first time.
epochs.imgDesc: Anatomy of an amniotic egg — key innovation for land reproduction.

epochs.name: Mississippian
epochs.desc: Almost the entire Northern Hemisphere flooded by shallow seas.

periods.name: Devonian
periods.desc: Age of fishes; first amphibians.

epochs.name: Late Devonian
epochs.desc: Tetrapods emerge on land; mass extinctions devastate marine life.
epochs.imgDesc: Tiktaalik — first fish to walk on land

epochs.name: Middle Devonian
epochs.desc: First forests appear; jawed fish evolve lung-like adaptations.
epochs.imgDesc: Dunkleosteus — armoured apex predator

epochs.name: Early Devonian
epochs.desc: Land plants, arthropods, jawed fish and ammonoids appear.
epochs.imgDesc: Pleuroceras solare — early Devonian ammonoid fossil.

periods.name: Silurian
periods.desc: Warm seas, reefs; first land plants.

epochs.name: Pridoli
epochs.desc: Terrestrial ecosystems expand with plants and arthropods.
epochs.imgCredit: Muséum national d'Histoire naturelle — CC BY 3.0

epochs.name: Ludlow
epochs.desc: Coral reefs expand; marine and land plant life diversifies.

epochs.name: Wenlock
epochs.desc: Marine life recovers; coral reef systems flourish.
epochs.imgDesc: Silurian seafloor — cephalopods, trilobites, crinoids and corals.

epochs.name: Llandovery
epochs.desc: Life recovers after extinction; seas rise and reef growth resumes.

periods.name: Ordovician
periods.desc: Complex marine life; mass extinction.

epochs.name: Late Ordovician
epochs.desc: Global glaciation triggers the massive Ordovician–Silurian mass extinction.

epochs.name: Middle Ordovician
epochs.desc: Seas flood continents; intense volcanism and tectonic activity.

epochs.name: Early Ordovician
epochs.desc: Great Biodiversification Event; marine life explodes in diversity.

periods.name: Cambrian
periods.desc: Marine animals with hard shells appear.

epochs.name: Furongian
epochs.desc: Trilobites diversify then collapse; Cambrian life ends.

epochs.name: Miaolingian
epochs.desc: Major trilobite extinctions; new species emerge after.

epochs.name: Series 2
epochs.desc: First trilobites appear; early Cambrian extinction event follows.

epochs.name: Terreneuvian
epochs.desc: Cambrian explosion begins; complex animal groups emerge.
epochs.imgDesc: Cambrian explosion — first complex animal groups emerge.

periods.name: Ediacaran
periods.desc: First complex soft-bodied multicellular animals appear.

periods.name: Cryogenian
periods.desc: Earth freezes over in Snowball Earth events.
periods.imgDesc: Paleogeographic map of Earth at 690 Ma — Snowball Earth.

periods.name: Tonian
periods.desc: Supercontinent Rodinia breaks apart; crust stretches and rifts.

periods.name: Stenian
periods.desc: Mountains form widely; supercontinent Rodinia coming together.

periods.name: Ectasian
periods.desc: Continental platforms expand; tectonic rifting begins worldwide.

periods.name: Calymmian
periods.desc: Supercontinent Columbia begins breaking apart.

periods.name: Statherian
periods.desc: Continents stabilize into cratons; first nucleated cells appear.

periods.name: Orosirian
periods.desc: Intense mountain-building shapes nearly every continent.
periods.imgDesc: Vredefort Dome — largest verified impact crater on Earth.

periods.name: Rhyacian
periods.desc: Widespread volcanic activity floods Earth's surface with lava.

periods.name: Siderian
periods.desc: Great Oxidation Event triggers first global glaciation.

eras.label: Eoarchean
eras.imgDesc: Acasta Gneiss — oldest exposed continental crust

eras.label: Late Hadean
eras.desc: Late Heavy Bombardment; possible prebiotic chemistry.
eras.imgDesc: Vredefort crater — largest known impact site
